title: COMPAT — Constraint-Market Compatibility: Plumbing, Heating & Air-Conditioning Contractors (NAICS 238220)

sections.title: Step 07 — Product Constraints (from upstream)
sections.content:
Constraints carried forward from 01_product_decomposition. No web search augmentation per instructions.

| # | Constraint | Type | Severity | Threshold | is_absolute |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Media Limitation | physical | hard | Water/water-like only | true |
| C2 | Maximum Medium Temperature | physical | hard | 90 °C | true |
| C3 | Maximum Operating Pressure | physical | hard | 10 bar | true |
| C4 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | operational | soft | 0.25–4.2 l/min (variant) | false |
| C5 | Air Bubble Tolerance | physical | soft | ~2% vol | false |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition | operational | hard | Pipe must be full | true |
| C7 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | operational | soft | 5–10× DN | false |
| C8 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | physical | soft | ~0.2% with NTC comp. | false |

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Constraint-Market Assessment: NAICS 238220
sections.content:
**Market context:** HVAC contractors install, commission, and maintain hydronic heating/cooling systems in residential and commercial buildings. Marquardt's sensor sits at L4.5.2.1 (Ultrasonic Inline Flow Sensor) under L5.5.2 Hydraulic Balancing & Flow Measurement within L6.5 Testing & Commissioning. [SRC: VN_plumbing_heating_and_air_conditioning_co]

Operating environment: Closed-loop hydronic circuits, DN15–DN25 pipe, water or water-glycol media, 5–80 °C, 1–4 bar typical (max 6 bar), flow rates 5–30 l/min per zone. [SRC: 05_market_discovery, VN]

### Verdict Matrix

| # | Constraint | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Media Limitation | **none** | Hydronic circuits use water or water-glycol mixtures — exact media match. No oils, gases, or non-polar liquids in HVAC hydronic systems. [SRC: 01, VN L5.5.2] |
| C2 | Max Medium Temperature (90 °C) | **none** | Residential/commercial hydronic circuits operate at 40–80 °C. High-temperature boilers reach 82 °C max flow temp. Well within 90 °C limit. [SRC: 05_market_discovery Rank 2] |
| C3 | Max Operating Pressure (10 bar) | **none** | Residential hydronic systems: 1–3 bar. Commercial: 2–6 bar. Safety relief valves set at 3 bar (residential) or 6 bar (commercial). Well within 10 bar. [SRC: VN L6.3–L6.5 context] |
| C4 | Min Flow Rate Detection | **none** | Zone commissioning flows are 5–30 l/min for DN15–DN25 circuits. Minimum detectable (4.2 l/min DN20, 0.25 l/min DN14) is below typical operating range. [SRC: 01 specs, 05 Rank 2] |
| C5 | Air Bubble Tolerance (~2% vol) | **none** | Standard commissioning sequence: fill → purge → pressure test → flow balance. Properly purged closed-loop hydronic systems have <0.5% air content by the time flow measurement occurs. Chronic air entrainment is a system defect, not a routine operating condition. [SRC: VN L5.5.1, L6.5] |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition | **none** | Closed-loop hydronic circuits are fully filled and pressurized as prerequisite to commissioning. Partially filled pipes are a fault state caught during pressure testing (L5.5.1) before flow balancing. [SRC: VN L5.5.1] |
| C7 | Min Straight Pipe Run (5–10× DN) | **mitigable** | Tight mechanical rooms and retrofit installations may have elbows, tees, or valves close to sensor insertion point. Achieving 100–250 mm straight run (5–10× DN20) is constrained in some installations. |
| C8 | Temp-Dependent Accuracy | **none** | HVAC systems operate in moderate range (5–80 °C) with gradual temperature changes. Built-in NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%. No extreme transients in standard hydronic operation. [SRC: 01 constraints] |

### C7 Mitigation Detail

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Mitigation actions** | 1. Specify sensor placement during system design (L6.2) to ensure adequate straight run upstream. 2. Install compact flow straightener upstream of sensor (<$50, adds 2× DN equivalent). 3. Use DN14 variant where space is constrained (shorter 120 mm body, lower straight-run requirement in absolute terms). |
| **Cost estimate** | low (<$10K) |
| **Time estimate** | 0 — addressable at installation with no product modification |

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total assessments** | 8 |
| **Knockouts** | 0 |
| **Mitigable** | 1 (C7 — Minimum Straight Pipe Run) |
| **None (irrelevant)** | 7 |
| **Market status** | **SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring |

**Conclusion:** NAICS 238220 presents zero physics or regulatory barriers to Marquardt's ultrasonic flow sensor. The only constraint with any relevance is installation-space-dependent straight pipe run (C7), mitigable at low cost via design-phase planning or a flow straightener. This aligns with the market's Tier 1 near-adjacent classification (architecture distance = 3) and confirms the sensor can enter this market with the current product, no redesign required. [SRC: 05_market_discovery Rank 2]

---

rows.Rationale: Hydronic circuits use water or water-glycol mixtures — exact media match. No oils, gases, or non-polar liquids in HVAC hydronic systems. [SRC: 01, VN L5.5.2]
Residential/commercial hydronic circuits operate at 40–80 °C. High-temperature boilers reach 82 °C max flow temp. Well within 90 °C limit. [SRC: 05_market_discovery Rank 2]
Residential hydronic systems: 1–3 bar. Commercial: 2–6 bar. Safety relief valves set at 3 bar (residential) or 6 bar (commercial). Well within 10 bar. [SRC: VN L6.3–L6.5 context]
Zone commissioning flows are 5–30 l/min for DN15–DN25 circuits. Minimum detectable (4.2 l/min DN20, 0.25 l/min DN14) is below typical operating range. [SRC: 01 specs, 05 Rank 2]

Standard commissioning sequence: fill → purge → pressure test → flow balance. Properly purged closed-loop hydronic systems have <0.5% air content by the time flow measurement occurs. Chronic air entrainment is a system defect, not a routine operating condition. [SRC: VN L5.5.1, L6.5]

Closed-loop hydronic circuits are fully filled and pressurized as prerequisite to commissioning. Partially filled pipes are a fault state caught during pressure testing (L5.5.1) before flow balancing. [SRC: VN L5.5.1]

Tight mechanical rooms and retrofit installations may have elbows, tees, or valves close to sensor insertion point. Achieving 100–250 mm straight run (5–10× DN20) is constrained in some installations.

HVAC systems operate in moderate range (5–80 °C) with gradual temperature changes. Built-in NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%. No extreme transients in standard hydronic operation. [SRC: 01 constraints]

rows.Value: 1. Specify sensor placement during system design (L6.2) to ensure adequate straight run upstream. 2. Install compact flow straightener upstream of sensor (<$50, adds 2× DN equivalent). 3. Use DN14 variant where space is constrained (shorter 120 mm body, lower straight-run requirement in absolute terms).
0 — addressable at installation with no product modification
**SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring

HVAC operates in moderate range (5-80 degC) with gradual changes. NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%.

properties.rationale: Tight mechanical rooms and retrofit installations may constrain straight-run distance. Addressable via design-phase sensor placement or flow straightener.
